skill.name: the-oracle
skill.description: Provides a structured workflow for deep research, architectural analysis, debugging investigations, and code review using a dedicated research agent.
skill.summary: Run structured, evidence-based investigations across code, logs, documentation, and external references.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution and flagged ordinary text as weak cryptography. Manual review found no executable shell commands, cryptographic implementation, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts in SKILL.md. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from broad research instructions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged triple backtick Markdown fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are documentation examples and prompt templates, not executable code, and no command invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fence delimiters or nearby documentation examples. I found no shell command, Ruby code, subprocess call, or user-controlled execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Ordinary Text Reported as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged text in the description and examples as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. Manual review found no MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, encryption routine, hashing routine, or cryptographic API use at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain natural language about research and architecture, not cryptographic code. No weak algorithm name or implementation is present in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Complex engineering questions often require code search, log review, documentation checks, and careful synthesis. This skill provides a structured research workflow for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Structures complex questions into core question, context, and success criteria.
Guides a dedicated research agent through planning, investigation, synthesis, and final reporting.
Supports codebase exploration with file search, pattern search, and full-file reading.
Includes optional web research for external documentation and references.
Produces answers with evidence, caveats, confidence levels, and recommended next steps.
limitations: It is a workflow guide, not an automated scanner or standalone tool.
Results depend on the quality of the context and access granted to the agent.
It may take longer than direct answers because it favors broad investigation.
Users should avoid sharing secrets, private keys, or sensitive logs unless necessary.

use_cases.title: Trace a difficult production bug
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to connect logs, symptoms, code paths, and likely root causes.

use_cases.title: Evaluate architecture before refactoring
use_cases.description: Ask for a structured review of current boundaries, dependencies, and practical refactoring steps.

use_cases.title: Review complex changes with context
use_cases.description: Investigate correctness, edge cases, performance risks, and maintainability before merge.

prompt_templates.title: Basic investigation
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have a clear question and a small amount of context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the oracle to answer this question: [question]. Context: [files, symptoms, or notes]. Success means a clear answer with evidence and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Debugging investigation
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when an error needs root cause analysis across logs and code paths.
prompt_templates.prompt: Ask the oracle to investigate this bug: [symptom or error]. Include these logs and related files: [context]. Map likely causes and recommend fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a design feels hard to maintain or needs a refactoring plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the oracle to analyze this architecture: [components or directories]. Identify coupling, unclear ownership, risks, and a practical refactoring path.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive technical review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a change needs deep review across correctness, design, and operational risk.
prompt_templates.prompt: Ask the oracle to review this work: [change summary and relevant files]. Evaluate correctness, edge cases, performance, security, and maintainability.

output_examples.input: Use the oracle to find why a background worker sometimes misses retries.
output_examples.output: A concise root cause report that lists the likely failure path, the files reviewed, evidence from logs, confidence level, and two recommended fixes.

output_examples.input: Ask the oracle whether this service boundary should be split.
output_examples.output: An architecture assessment covering current coupling, ownership problems, migration risks, and a staged plan for improving the boundary.

output_examples.input: Use the oracle to review the code we just wrote.
output_examples.output: A review summary with correctness issues, missing edge cases, performance concerns, and prioritized changes before merge.

best_practices: Provide the core question, relevant files, logs, and what a successful answer should include.
Limit the scope when possible so the investigation remains focused and useful.
Review evidence and caveats before acting on recommendations.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for simple factual questions that need a direct answer.
Do not provide secrets, tokens, or private credentials as investigation context.
Do not ask for broad research without a clear success criterion.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It turns complex engineering questions into structured research tasks with evidence-based answers.

faq.question: Does it run code or commands?
faq.answer: The skill file does not define executable commands. It provides prompts for investigation using available agent tools.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it for debugging, architecture analysis, refactoring plans, complex code reviews, and multi-source research.

faq.question: Can it use web research?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow allows web research when external documentation or references are needed.

faq.question: What should I include in my prompt?
faq.answer: Include the main question, relevant context, constraints, what you tried, and the expected outcome.

faq.question: Is it suitable for sensitive data?
faq.answer: Use caution. Remove secrets and only share sensitive logs or files when they are necessary for the task.
